items.title: Longtime Trail Blazers broadcaster Kevin Calabro parts ways with team due to 'subprime' offer
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers made headlines in May after the organization laid off about 70 people as part of a restructuring under new team owner Tom Dundon. The negative press kept coming Wednesday, when The Oregonian's Joe Freeman reported that longtime broadcaster Kevin Calabro was parting ways with the organization.Calabro wasn't the only one. He left under his own terms, but Forbes' Maury Brown also reported that "TV analyst Michael Holton and statistician Tom Haberstroh, as well as TV hosts Neil Everett and Jamie Hudson have all been let go."(Editor's note: Tom Haberstroh is a contributing writer at Yahoo Sports)Portland released a statement about the cuts to its broadcast teams. "We are deeply grateful to all the hard-working individuals who have contributed to our award-winning broadcasts over the years. I can't comment on individual personnel…

items.title: Did Adam Silver's remarks delay LeBron James' free agency choice?
items.summary: There's a logical explanation to why it's taking so long for LeBron James to announce his free agency decision on where he will play in the 2026-27 season.A Cleveland sports radio host believes that James has intentionally pushed back his announcement because he was "irritated" with comments made last week by NBA Commissioner Adam Silver.Cleveland sports radio personality Andy Baskin said Silver's comments "irritated" James and the former Los Angeles Lakers star decided to push back the announcement. Baskin, who has a radio show called Baskin and Phelps on 92.3 The Fan, went as far as to say that James was ready to announce his decision last week."LeBron's crew was probably ready to make an announcement, especially because they were in New York," Baskin said. "The world had their eyes on New York because of the World Cup as well. Yeah,…

items.title: Grizzlies, D'Angelo Russell expected to work together to allow veteran guard to find new home
items.summary: Grizzlies point guard D'Angelo Russell is not believed to be a long-term piece in Memphis, with both sides expected to work together toward a resolution that would allow the 30-year-old to find a new home at some point this offseason — whether via a buyout agreement or a trade — sources tell Yahoo Sports.Russell, who appeared in just 25 games last season in Dallas, arrived in Memphis earlier this month as part of an expansive six-team deal involving Milwaukee, Washington, Dallas, the LA Clippers and Detroit. The trade also sent center Isaiah Stewart, guard AJ Johnson, three future second-round picks, a conditional first-round pick and a second-round swap to the Grizzlies.The veteran guard was assumed to be part of a ball-handling group in Dallas last season designed to assist in the development of then-rookie Cooper Flagg, but started just three games — seemingly falling out of…

items.title: The Indecision: What's LeBron James waiting for?
items.summary: LeBron James has never simply signed somewhere. In 2010, he joined the Heat because Dwyane Wade was already in place and they acquired Chris Bosh. In 2014, he returned home to Cleveland to join Kyrie Irving with the understanding that the first overall draft pick was getting flipped for Kevin Love. In 2018, he signed with the Lakers knowing Year 1 was a bridge to a blockbuster. The next summer, Anthony Davis arrived. Three free agency decisions all had one common thread: LeBron signed up for a team with a plan.This brings us to the summer of 2026. The popular theory around the league is that LeBron has been slow-playing his decision to see if any of his suitors could find a way to land Irving or Davis. If a team proved capable of acquiring one of his championship teammates, sources say, it might sway him in that direction.Nobody has been able or willing to do it yet. The Irving path…

items.title: Ty Lawson, former Nuggets star, arrested for shoplifting
items.summary: Ty Lawson, a former Denver Nuggets player, was arrested.According to an arrest affidavit obtained by FOX31, Lawson allegedly stole a bottle of vodka from a store in Castle Rock, Colorado on July 16.Former Denver Nuggets player Ty Lawson was arrested on July 16. A shop owner called police about a man who entered the store and stole a bottle of Tito’s Vodka before leaving at approximately 8:03 p.m. She told police the man took a drink from the bottle before walking to a nearby restaurant, Scileppi’s, per the arrest affidavit.The shop owner also revealed she witnessed Lawson stumbling and believed he was intoxicated. The man then sat at the bar with the stolen Tito’s bottle.The man was later identified as Tywon Lawson. He faces a shoplifting charge since it was a theft less than $300. Lawson’s friend, who was found holding the Tito’s bottle, was also detained but later released after…

items.title: What will decide if Lakers trade Bronny James to LeBron’s next team
items.summary: Bronny and LeBron JamesSince LeBron James announced he wasn’t going to return to the Lakers in late June, there have been questions around the NBA whether his son, Bronny James, will follow him to his next team. While LeBron and Bronny could be teammates for the 2026-27 season on LeBron’s next team, it also isn’t guaranteed the Lakers will trade the younger James to his father’s next destination. As has been described to the California Post, there’s a real possibility the Lakers will keep Bronny on their roster going into next season, even as they continue to tinker with their roster to be compliant with the league’s roster rules before Opening Night in October. Bronny James still may have a future with the Lakers with LeBron leaving. Corey Sipkin for NY PostIf the team signing LeBron is also looking to acquire Bronny, they would likely need to send the Lakers a real…

items.title: LeBron James ‘irritated’ by Adam Silver pressure
items.summary: LeBron James was reportedly prepared to make his free agency announcement last week. Then, NBA commissioner Adam Silver publicly revealed the 41-year-old’s delayed decision is the reason why the league’s schedule hasn’t been finalized. Silver’s comments seemingly played a role in the ongoing saga. On 92.3 The Fan’s Baskin and Phelps show, Cleveland radio host Andy Baskin revealed what a source shared about James’ free agency decision.LeBron James made an appearance at Fanatics Fest in New York. Ryan Rolo/Shutterstock“Here’s what I was told yesterday about the whole LeBron situation. Last week, LeBron’s crew was probably ready to make an announcement, especially because they were in New York,” Baskin said. “The world had their eyes on New York because of the World Cup as well. “They were probably comfortable making an announcement as early as last week. And then, people were telling them…
